---
title: "Assertiveness for Recovery Boundaries"
format: html
---
# Assertiveness for Recovery Boundaries

Assertiveness is the ability to communicate clearly and respectfully without giving up your values. In recovery, assertiveness protects sobriety when others pressure you to drink or when you need to set limits around risky situations.

People often confuse assertiveness with aggression. Aggression pushes or attacks; assertiveness is direct, calm, and boundaried.

Assertiveness protects recovery by reducing interpersonal pressure and ambiguity. Clear communication of boundaries lowers decision strain and decreases exposure to high-risk situations.

## Boundary Script Practice
Write and practice three statements:
1. "No thanks, I don't drink."
2. "I'm leaving early tonight."
3. "I'm focused on my health and recovery."

::: {.callout-important}
### Practice plan

Work through this in real-life situations rather than only reading it.

Use one assertive statement in a real situation this week and record the outcome.

If something feels hard, simplify the step and repeat it consistently.
:::
